methods.name: dxtr.cochains.cochain.Cochain.value_closest_to
documentation: Gets the value closest to a provided position.

A cochain data structure.

A discrete version of the mathematical notion of differential k-form.
Defined on a discrete version of the notion of manifold.

Attributes
----------
_complex : dxtr.complexes.simplicialcomplex
    The simplicial complex the cochain is defined on.
_dim : int
    The topoligical dimension at which the cochain is defined.
_val : numpy.ndarray(float)
    The value of the cochain on the associated simplices.
_dual : Bool
    Default is False. If True the cochain is defined on the dual complex.

Notes
-----
  * The values of the `Cochain` can be scalars or vectors.
  * We can also defined values as sparse matrices but this is mostly used 
  to define differential operators on a whole `SimplicialComplex`.
  * TODO: Would it be relevant to allow for Cochain definition on 
    AbstractSimplicialComplex ?
  * TODO: In the current state, no verification of the length of 
    the input values is done... Should it be added ?...

The main data classes of the library into pyvista.UnstructuredGrid objects
and to instanciate `dxtr` classes from ply and vtk files.
* Enable the users to save the `SimplicialComplex` and `Cochain` objects
on the disk at the vtk format so they can reuse them easily within
.  other frameworks, e.g. Paraview for visualisation purposes.
* Enable visualization of these objects using the pyvista library,
See accompanying file LICENSE.txt or copy at

functions.name: dxtr.utils.wrappers._ugrid_from_simplicialcomplex
documentation:
Convert a SimplicialComplex into a pv.UnstructuredGrid.

Notes
-----
* If `smallest_cell_dim` is 0, only the top simplices are recorded.
* Otherwise, all k-simplices with k in [smallest_cell_dim, complex.dim]
  are recorded.
* This argument is useful when recording k-Cochains (see method 
  `_ugrid_from_cochain`) for we need to define all simplices of higher 
  dim and attribute them a `nan` value.
Usefull functions for the UGrid class #

functions.name: dxtr.utils.wrappers._ugrid_from_dual_simplicialcomplex
documentation:
Convert a Dual `SimplicialComplex` into a `pv.UnstructuredGrid`.

Notes
-----
  * If `smallest_cell_dim` is 0, only the top dual cells are recorded.
  * Otherwise, all k-cells with k in [smallest_cell_dim, manifold.dim]
    are recorded.
  * This argument is useful when recording dual k-Cochains (see method 
    `_ugrid_from_cochain`) for we need to define all simplices of higher 
    dim and attribute them a `nan` value.
  * The `elif` condition is ment to deal with boundaries of open simplicial 
    manifold : The dual complex is ill-defined on the border and has n-1 
    cells with only 1 cofaces. For those, we assign twice their single 
    coface to avoid glitches in the visualization.

functions.name: dxtr.utils.wrappers._ugrid_from_cochain
documentation:
Convert a `Cochain` into a `pv.UnstructuredGrid`.

Notes
-----
  * The `scaling_factor` argument is useful for visualization proposes. 
    Notably, to increase of decrease the size of vectors one wants to
    visualize.

functions.name: dxtr.utils.wrappers._format_cochain_values
documentation:
Formats cochain so they can be used as data for `UnstructuredGrid`. 

Parameters
----------
cochain
    The `Cochain` to format.
scaling_factor
    Optional, default is 1. A multiplicator to apply uniformly to all
    the values of the `Cochain`. See Notes.
for_visualization
  Optional, default is False. If True the scalar values on 1-`Cochain` are
  converted into vectors to enable the drawing of edges.

Notes
-----
  * The `scaling_factor` argument is useful for visualization proposes. 
    Notably, to increase of decrease the size of vectors one wants to
    visualize.
  * The purpose of this method: For k-cochains wiht 0<k<n, 
    their values will be stored as 'cell_data' in the instantiated 
    `UnstructuredGrid`.
  * 'cell_data' gathers all data that not defined on vertices 
    (i.e. edges data, faces data, volumes data). A cochain contains only one 
    of these types. But we need to provide values for all of them. So this 
    method fills the undesired cell_data to the NAN value.
  * Afterwards, when visualizing these cochains in Paraview, one only need 
    to set the color of NAN values to transparent.

modules.name: dxtr.utils.quality_control
documentation: This submodule contains useful functions to assess the quality
of the simplices forming a simplicial complex.
See accompanying file LICENSE.txt or copy at

functions.name: dxtr.utils.quality_control.simplex_shape_regularity_score
documentation:
Quantifies the regularity of a simplex.

Parameters
----------
splx : 
    The simplex to analyze.

Returns
-------
A float between 0 and 1 defined as: 
    1 - sdt(edge_lengths)/mean(edge_lengths)

Notes
-----
  * A score of 1 corresponds to the most regular simplex; *i.e.* one with
    all its edges of equal length.
  * This measure is meaningful only of k-simplices with k > 2.
    Therefore, we return 1 of all k-simplices with k < 2.

functions.name: dxtr.utils.quality_control.shape_regularity_score
documentation:
Quantifies the regularity of a simplicial complex.

Parameters:
-----------
cplx:
    The n-simplicial complex to consider.
return_scores:
    If true, returns an array with the regularity scores of all n-simplices.
    Else returns only the median value and the standard deviation.

Returns
-------
    (Median, standard deviation) | array of all n-simplex regularity scores.

functions.name: dxtr.utils.quality_control.well_centered_score
documentation:
Quantifies how well-centered are simplices within a manifold.

Parameters
----------
mfld:
    The simplicial manifold to investigate.

return_scores:
    returns scores of all n-simplices if True.
verbose:
  Prints a summary if True.

Returns
-------
The ratio of well-centered n-simplices within the n-manifold and
the scores (optional).

Notes
-----
* We arbitrary consider well-centered simplices 
  with circumcenter lying on an edge (*i.e.* one of its 
  barycentric coordinate vanishes). This is debatable.
* In its current version, this method does not quantify how much 
  the ill-centered simplices are ill-centered.
* The individual score of ill-centered simplices corresponds to the negative 
  barycentric coordinate. The more negative it is a worst the triangle is.

functions.name: dxtr.utils.quality_control.complex_quality
documentation:
Computes various regularity estimators on a simplicial complex.

Parameters
----------
complex:
    The n- simplicial complex to analyze.

Returns
-------
A pandas DataFrame containing regularity estimator values 
for each n-simplex of the considered n-complex.

Note
-----
List of the regularity estimators computed:
* regularity score
* well-centeredness score
